description: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features used in configuration, log messages, and parameters do not protect against attacker-controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints (Log4Shell). Allows remote code execution.
Spring Framework RCE via Data Binding on JDK 9+ (Spring4Shell). A non-trivial deployment with Spring MVC may be vulnerable to remote code execution through data binding.
Apache Tomcat AJP Connector flaw (Ghostcat) allows reading or including arbitrary files from the web application, potentially leading to remote code execution.
Windows Print Spooler remote code execution vulnerability (PrintNightmare). An attacker can run arbitrary code with SYSTEM privileges.
The SMBv1 server in Microsoft Windows allows remote code execution via crafted packets (EternalBlue). Used by the WannaCry ransomware.
Race condition in the Linux kernel memory subsystem (Dirty COW) allows local users to gain write access to read-only memory mappings and escalate privileges.
Path traversal and file disclosure in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.49. With certain configurations this can lead to remote code execution.
OpenSSH allows remote attackers to enumerate valid usernames by observing differences in authentication responses.
